prompt: 2026: kolm.ai's CUSTOMER is the company that SHIPS an AI agent/LLM feature and must clear an enterprise buyer's SECURITY REVIEW to close the deal. Segment these customers precisely and for EACH give (cite real posts/data 2025-2026): (1) segment name + size signal; (2) the JOB TO BE DONE; (3) the TRIGGER event that makes them buy NOW (first enterprise prospect, a security questionnaire lands in the inbox, a SOC 2 demand, an EU AI Act/Art-12 deadline, a failed deal); (4) what they do TODAY to handle the AI portion (ignore it, fill the questionnaire by hand, buy a pentest, point at SOC 2, use Vanta); (5) who is the buyer + budget authority (founder, CTO, head of security, GRC lead); (6) willingness to pay for a signed AI audit artifact and price sensitivity; (7) urgency 1-5. Segments to consider: seed/pre-A AI startup selling to enterprise; Series A-B AI-native SaaS; incumbent SaaS bolting on an AI agent; AI agent platform/infra vendor; regulated-vertical AI (fintech/healthtech/legal). Be concrete, quote founders/buyers.

2026: what makes a SECURITY/COMPLIANCE product sell OFF-THE-SHELF / self-serve (credit-card, no sales call) vs sales-led? Cite real examples and price points: which security/compliance/devtool products convert self-serve (Snyk, Semgrep, Vanta self-serve tier, Cobalt entry, Drata, Secureframe, oneleet, delve), at what price ceiling does self-serve break and a sales motion take over (is $750 one-time self-serve? is $299-$999/mo self-serve? where does $15k-$25k force a call?). What EXACTLY must a buyer see on the page to purchase a $750 signed security artifact without talking to anyone (proof, sample report, instant value, money-back, trust signals, a verifiable demo)? Quote founders on PLG-for-security and the self-serve-to-enterprise handoff. What kills self-serve trust for a security product?

2026 DEMAND side: when a startup hands an enterprise security reviewer a SIGNED, offline-verifiable AI security audit (not a SOC 2, a NEW kind of artifact), what makes the reviewer ACCEPT it and let the deal proceed vs ignore it and send their own 200-question questionnaire anyway? Cite real reviewer/CISO/TPRM behavior 2025-2026: do reviewers accept supplementary vendor-provided evidence to SHORTCUT a questionnaire? Which TPRM platforms (Whistic, UpGuard, VisoTrust, SecurityScorecard, Drata vendor-risk) ingest vendor-provided artifacts? What artifact format/framing gets accepted (mapped to their questionnaire, mapped to SOC2/ISO/NIST, a trust-center link)? The key question: does the vendor buying kolm actually SAVE TIME / WIN THE DEAL, and what proof of that exists? Quote real buyers.

2026: what CATEGORY language and search terms do buyers actually use for the kolm problem? Do AI startups search/budget for 'AI security audit', 'AI penetration test', 'AI compliance', 'agent security', 'LLM security assessment', 'AI TPRM / vendor evidence', 'AI red team report'? Which term has budget attached and buying intent vs which is just awareness? What do the closest competitors (Vanta AI, Cobalt, HiddenLayer, Mindgard, the emerging signed-artifact startups Proxara/Forge) call their offering in their headline? What is the BEST category name + one-line positioning for an on-demand, signed, buyer-verifiable AI security audit that a buyer would immediately understand and pay for? Give 5 candidate category names ranked by clarity + existing budget. Cite real 2026 sources.
